screenshot.limitation: The original baseline capture did not move across a concrete candidate cell; the added multilingual screenshot demonstrates dictionary detail rendering.

screenshots.limitation: Playwright hover/move/touch automation did not display the dictionary panel, so no screenshot file was created.

screenshots.captureMethod: Physical mouse movement across the first candidate cell after typing `nei`.

sourceEvidence.evidence: DictionaryPanel renders entry head, body, other data rows, and More Languages rows when CandidateInfo has dictionary entries.
CandidateEntry exposes honzi, Jyutping, part-of-speech, register, labels, definitions, written/vernacular/collocation rows, canonical reference, pronunciation notes, and other language definitions.
Language names, part-of-speech labels, reading notes, registers, labels, and More Languages display names are mapped here.
dictionary-panel, entry-head, entry-body, pos, lbl, th, and td styles define dictionary visual structure.

dictionaryFields.rendering: entry-head large Chinese text with Kai fallback over Sung or Hei

dictionaryFields.example: changed tone 變音, semantic reading 訓讀, creative reading 生造音, literary/colloquial reading notes when present

dictionaryFields.rendering: definition span in main display language

dictionaryFields.example: Hindi, Indonesian, Nepali, Urdu rows when enabled and populated
dictionaryFields.rendering: table with caption More Languages, language name in th, meaning in td

dictionaryFields.example: Written Form 書面語, Vernacular Form 口語, Collocation 配搭

dictionaryFields.rendering: body reference text instead of a direct definition

sampleEntryFromRuntimeCandidate.partOfSpeech: available when lookup-filter source data supplies partOfSpeech
sampleEntryFromRuntimeCandidate.readingNotes: available when source data supplies pronunciation labels or literary/colloquial reading data

moreLanguages.source: DictionaryPanel.tsx renders More Languages rows from CandidateEntry.otherLanguages(preferences) for enabled non-main display languages.

visualTokens.panel: dictionary-panel uses base-300 background, rounded-md, px-5 pt-3 pb-4, text-[12pt], whitespace-normal, min-w-48.
visualTokens.caption: More Languages caption uses font-medium text-[13pt]

limitations: The original baseline dictionary-detail screenshot remains partial because the first automation did not move across a concrete candidate cell.
A multilingual Indonesian-main dictionary screenshot was captured after moving the mouse over the first candidate cell.
The sample entry is not a complete dictionary database dump.
validationNote: JSON is stored as readable UTF-8 with BOM for Windows PowerShell 5.1 compatibility. Prefer Get-Content -Raw -Encoding UTF8 when validating.
